title: Topological magnon insulator spin excitations in the two-dimensional ferromagnet CrBr$_3$
type1_summary: No clearly identifiable cleaned, replot-ready dataset files were provided in the inspection report.
type2_summary: No clearly identifiable raw/unprocessed instrument data files were provided in the inspection report.
classification_notes: The paper itself clearly contains both raw experimental measurements and processed/source-style figure data in the publication, but the provided file inventory includes only one PDF report. Without content inspection, it cannot be assigned to Type 1 or Type 2 conservatively.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper mentions Supplementary Materials for details on lattice structure, magnetization/specific heat, constant-energy contours, and model calculations, but no explicit repository or dataset description matching this PDF is provided.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Single large PDF (14.6 MB) from Zenodo; no internal structure, tables, or machine-readable data were provided in the inspection report. The filename suggests a report rather than a data table or raw instrument output.
file_classifications.reasoning: This file is best classified as documentation rather than Type 1 or Type 2 data because the available evidence indicates a report-style PDF, not a directly plottable dataset or raw measurement file. There is insufficient file-content evidence to identify it as figure-ready data or raw experimental output.
file_classifications.ambiguity: High ambiguity: the PDF content was not inspected, so it could contain supplementary narrative, figures, or embedded data. Conservatively treated as documentation.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies spin excitations in the two-dimensional ferromagnet CrBr3 using inelastic neutron scattering on single crystals, combined with first-principles-based model calculations. The main result is that the expected Dirac crossing at the K point is actually gapped by about 3.5 meV, and the data are best described by a Heisenberg model with next-nearest-neighbor Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction and single-ion anisotropy, making CrBr3 a topological magnon insulator with magnon bands of Chern numbers +1 and -1.
measurement_types: Raman spectroscopy (referenced from prior/ supplementary discussion)
first-principles electronic structure / Hubbard-model-derived exchange calculations

figures.description: Schematic crystal structure of CrBr3 with spin directions and exchange couplings J1, J2, J3 labeled.
Schematic Brillouin zone on the (h,k,0) plane with high-symmetry points and scan directions.
INS intensity map along the (h,h) direction at 6 K, combining low- and high-energy measurements, with calculated dispersions overlaid.
INS intensity map along the (h-0.5, h+0.5) direction at 6 K, showing acoustic and optical magnon branches and the K-point gap.
INS intensity map along the (-k,k) direction at 6 K, showing the optical branch dispersion.
Constant-Q cut at a K point from Fig. 1c showing a double-peak profile used to estimate the gap.
Constant-Q cut at a K point from Fig. 1d showing the same gap feature and peak separation.
INS spectrum along the [001] direction showing no meaningful out-of-plane dispersion above background, used to bound interlayer coupling.

Calculated exchange parameters J1, J2, J3, Kitaev K, off-diagonal Γ and Γ', NNN DM interaction A, and single-ion anisotropy Dz as functions of Hund's coupling JH/U, with a vertical line marking the chosen material parameters.
Side-by-side comparison of experimental INS spectra and calculated spectra from the J1-J2-J3-A-Dz model along the same momentum directions, with Chern numbers labeled for the magnon bands.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include time-of-flight inelastic neutron scattering spectra collected on MERLIN at ISIS from 40 coaligned CrBr3 single crystals (about 1.06 g total) at 6 K using incident energies Ei = 30 meV and 10.7 meV, plus powder X-ray diffraction at 300 K, magnetization, and specific heat measurements. The paper also references prior INS and ferromagnetic resonance results for the zone-center gap.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'See Supplementary Materials at URL for details on the determination of the lattice structure, magnetization and specific heat measurement, constant-energy contours of the spin excitations, and calculations of the eﬀective model, spin waves and Chern numbers.' No explicit public repository, downloadable dataset, or code archive is mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data are described at the instrument level rather than as files: time-of-flight neutron scattering data from MERLIN, with sample orientation, incident energies, chopper frequency, and energy resolution specified. Powder XRD, magnetization, and specific heat were also measured, but no file formats or repository locations are given.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include reciprocal-space intensity maps, constant-Q cuts used to extract the K-point gap, constant-energy contour maps mentioned in the supplement, and fitted exchange parameters summarized in Table I. The paper also reports derived quantities such as the gap energy (~3.5 meV), the upper limit on out-of-plane dispersion, and Chern numbers from model calculations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly present in the main text as INS intensity maps, line cuts, and theory overlays. The paper does not explicitly state that source data files for figures are provided, but the figures are based on processed experimental spectra and calculated dispersions. Supplementary figures are referenced for additional contour maps and characterization data.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned. Theoretical calculations are described conceptually (five-orbital Hubbard model, linear spin-wave theory, Chern number calculation), but no scripts are provided in the text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental-theoretical study. The main experimental dataset is neutron scattering intensity versus momentum and energy, processed into dispersion maps and constant-Q cuts. Theoretical outputs include exchange parameters from first-principles-derived Hubbard modeling and spin-wave dispersions/Chern numbers from an effective Hamiltonian. The paper emphasizes that the K-point gap is extracted from fitted peak separations in line cuts, and that finite intensity in the gap region may be resolution leakage rather than true band crossing. Supplementary materials are important for lattice determination, constant-energy contours, and model calculations, but their actual contents are not fully visible in the provided text.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct INS measurements on single crystals at MERLIN, plus powder XRD, magnetization, and specific heat measurements, indicating collection of raw experimental data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents processed INS intensity maps, constant-Q cuts, fitted gap values, Table I exchange parameters, and theory-overlaid dispersions, which are figure-ready/source-like data products.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “See Supplementary Materials at URL for details on the determination of the lattice structure, magnetization and specific heat measurement, constant-energy contours of the spin excitations, and calculations of the eﬀective model, spin waves and Chern numbers.”

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
